title: anatherum virginicum subvar. nashianum
intro: Anatherum virginicum subvar. nashianum, also known as Indian grass or big bluestem, is a type of perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its tall stalks and blue-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high silica content. Consuming Indian grass is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in herbal remedies for skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
